798d71ae-178b-4f6b-bd3d-c3b58bd2013b.name: ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning)
examplePlatforms: Aptean Process Manufacturing ERP – Ross Edition
798d71ae-178b-4f6b-bd3d-c3b58bd2013b.description: ERP is the system of record for the plant's commercial and planning data: purchase orders, sales orders, inventory balances, cost accounting, and the master production schedule all live here, and every other plant system (MES, WMS, LIMS, QMS) either feeds it or reads from it. In food and beverage manufacturing specifically, a generic ERP is not enough - the platform needs process-manufacturing extensions for recipe/formula-based production (rather than discrete bill-of-materials assembly), catch weight (billing and inventory tracked by actual measured weight, not just unit count), FEFO-driven inventory allocation, lot-level traceability through transformation and blending, and by-product/co-product yield accounting. Implementations typically integrate ERP with MES for shop-floor execution data and with WMS for warehouse-level inventory movement.

capabilities.name: Purchase and sales order management
capabilities.description: Tracks purchase orders to suppliers and sales orders to customers, tying procurement and fulfillment to the plant's commercial records.

capabilities.name: Inventory and cost accounting
capabilities.description: Maintains inventory balances and cost accounting records, the financial and quantity system of record for materials and finished goods.

capabilities.name: Master production schedule
capabilities.description: Holds the plant's production plan, driving what is scheduled to run and when.

capabilities.name: Recipe/formula and catch weight support
capabilities.description: Process-manufacturing extensions that handle formula-based production and variable (catch) weight products, which a generic ERP does not natively support.

capabilities.name: FEFO-driven inventory allocation
capabilities.description: Allocates inventory for orders based on first-expired-first-out logic rather than simple FIFO, required for perishable food inventory.

capabilities.name: Lot-level traceability and co-product/by-product yield accounting
capabilities.description: Tracks lots through production and accounts for yield across primary, co-, and by-products, supporting recall and traceability requirements.

3628303b-f98e-4dc6-8627-9ad94b5af3dd.name: MES (Manufacturing Execution System)
3628303b-f98e-4dc6-8627-9ad94b5af3dd.description: MES sits between ERP's plan and the physical plant floor. It dispatches ERP-generated work orders to specific lines, captures real-time production and downtime data from PLCs and line sensors, and builds the electronic batch record as production runs, tying together what was produced, on which equipment, by whom, and against which recipe parameters. On the quality side, MES is typically where CCP monitoring data (temperatures, pressures, timings) gets captured at the point of the process step rather than on paper, and where OEE (availability, performance, quality) is calculated from the same downtime and output data. Because it talks directly to plant-floor automation (PLCs, SCADA), MES is the integration point that turns physical equipment events into the digital records ERP, QMS, and traceability systems consume.

capabilities.name: Work order dispatch
capabilities.description: Receives ERP-generated work orders and dispatches them to specific lines/equipment for execution.

capabilities.name: Real-time production and downtime capture
capabilities.description: Pulls production counts, equipment status, and downtime events directly from PLCs and line sensors as they occur.

capabilities.name: Electronic batch record
capabilities.description: Assembles the record of what was produced, on which equipment, by whom, and against which recipe parameters as the run happens.

capabilities.name: CCP monitoring data capture
capabilities.description: Captures critical-control-point readings (temperatures, pressures, timings) at the point of the process step rather than on paper.

capabilities.name: OEE calculation
capabilities.description: Calculates overall equipment effectiveness (availability, performance, quality) from the same downtime and output data MES already captures.

39da6cf5-44d6-44d3-b613-d86a3530a7b1.name: WMS (Warehouse Management System)
39da6cf5-44d6-44d3-b613-d86a3530a7b1.description: WMS governs the physical movement and storage of inventory within the four walls of the warehouse, distinct from ERP's higher-level inventory accounting. It directs receiving and putaway (which dock door, which rack location), drives pick sequencing for outbound orders using FEFO logic so that closer-to-expiry lots ship first, reconciles physical inventory counts against system records (cycle counts), and generates the pick/pack/load instructions and shipping documentation that feed the Advance Ship Notice. WMS typically holds the location-level detail (pallet, rack, bin) that ERP does not track at that granularity, and hands lot and location data back to ERP or a dedicated traceability platform to support genealogy queries.

capabilities.name: Receiving and putaway
capabilities.description: Directs incoming product to a specific dock door and rack/bin location as it enters the warehouse.

capabilities.name: FEFO pick sequencing
capabilities.description: Drives outbound pick order using first-expired-first-out logic so closer-to-expiry lots ship first.

capabilities.name: Cycle counts
capabilities.description: Reconciles physical inventory counts against system records on an ongoing basis rather than a single annual count.

capabilities.name: Pick/pack/load and ASN generation
capabilities.description: Produces the pick/pack/load instructions and shipping documentation that feed the Advance Ship Notice sent to the receiving customer.

06ff688b-147f-4fde-846c-35ad49d333bc.name: QMS / Food Safety Software
06ff688b-147f-4fde-846c-35ad49d333bc.description: QMS / food safety software is the digital home for the plant's food-safety and quality management system: the HACCP plan itself, the CCP monitoring and corrective-action records generated as production runs, CAPA workflows for nonconformances, document and record control (SOPs, specs, training records), internal audit management, and often supplier qualification data as well. Where MES captures process data at the point of the line, QMS is usually where that data (and manually entered checks that have no PLC source) gets reviewed, exception-managed, and retained as the auditable record a regulator or third-party certification auditor will ask to see. Because HACCP, supplier management, and labeling all generate records that need retention and review, QMS platforms in this space commonly span several of those domains rather than specializing in just one.

capabilities.name: HACCP Plan Management
capabilities.description: Digital storage and version control of the written HACCP plan, including hazard analysis, CCPs, critical limits, and monitoring procedures.

capabilities.name: CCP Monitoring & Corrective Action
capabilities.description: Capture of real-time or logged monitoring data at critical control points, with workflows to record deviations and the corrective actions taken.

capabilities.name: CAPA Workflows
capabilities.description: Structured corrective and preventive action tracking - root cause analysis, assigned remediation tasks, and verification of effectiveness.

capabilities.name: Document & Record Control
capabilities.description: Version-controlled repository for SOPs, policies, and other controlled documents, with approval routing and record retention.

capabilities.name: Internal Audit
capabilities.description: Scheduling, checklist execution, and findings tracking for internal self-inspections against food safety and quality standards.

capabilities.name: Supplier Qualification Data
capabilities.description: Storage and tracking of supplier approval documentation, certifications, and ongoing supplier performance/compliance records.

0aa50349-ec76-4ea4-b24d-bd61159fff84.name: LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System)
0aa50349-ec76-4ea4-b24d-bd61159fff84.description: LIMS manages the lifecycle of a lab sample: logging intake (what material, what lot, what test panel is required), tracking the sample through analytical testing (microbiological, chemical, physical), capturing the resulting test values, and automatically comparing those values against the specification limits defined for that material or product. LIMS typically drives the hold/release decision that gates whether a lot can move to the next step (e.g., a raw material can't be used, or a finished lot can't ship, until LIMS shows all required results within spec), and it is the system most directly upstream of the Lab Test Result and Certificate of Analysis records used elsewhere in the plant.

capabilities.name: Sample intake
capabilities.description: Logs incoming samples with identifying metadata such as sample type, source lot, collection date/time, and the test panel to be run.

capabilities.name: Test assignment and tracking
capabilities.description: Assigns required analytical tests to a sample and tracks each test's status (pending, in progress, complete) through the lab workflow.

capabilities.name: Result capture
capabilities.description: Records analytical results entered manually or pulled from lab instruments, tied back to the originating sample and test.

capabilities.name: Spec-limit comparison
capabilities.description: Compares captured results against applicable specification limits to flag in-spec versus out-of-spec outcomes.

capabilities.name: Hold/release decisioning
capabilities.description: Uses the spec comparison outcome to drive disposition of the associated lot - holding it pending review or releasing it for the next step.

73835e3c-9d70-49bf-b855-e25139be8426.name: Supplier Management / Portal
73835e3c-9d70-49bf-b855-e25139be8426.description: A supplier management/portal system runs the ongoing lifecycle of qualifying and monitoring suppliers: it collects and stores qualification documents such as facility audits and GFSI-recognized certifications, gathers per-shipment COAs, and structures the ongoing monitoring workflows (re-audit reminders, certification expiration tracking, performance scorecards) that keep a supplier's status current rather than qualified once and forgotten. Because supplier qualification and monitoring are audit-facing obligations under FSMA's Preventive Controls supplier-verification requirements, this system is often the evidence source a plant points to when a regulator or customer audit asks how a given supplier was verified.

capabilities.name: Supplier qualification lifecycle
capabilities.description: Tracks a supplier from initial onboarding through approval, including collection of required qualification documentation before a supplier is approved to ship.

capabilities.name: Audit/certification document collection
capabilities.description: Intake and storage of third-party audit reports and certification documents (e.g. GFSI certificates), typically with expiration dates tracked against the source document.

capabilities.name: COA intake
capabilities.description: Collection and retention of Certificates of Analysis submitted by suppliers for incoming lots, tied to specific shipments or ingredient lots.

capabilities.name: Re-audit reminders
capabilities.description: Automated alerts triggered as a supplier's audit or certification approaches its expiration date, prompting renewal before it lapses.

capabilities.name: Expiration tracking
capabilities.description: Ongoing monitoring of validity dates across all supplier-submitted documents so expired records are flagged rather than silently relied upon.

capabilities.name: Supplier scorecards
capabilities.description: Ongoing performance monitoring that aggregates supplier-related metrics into a recurring rating used for approval status decisions.

22e46c8f-cf7e-4d53-b5f2-56c939ee76ff.name: Traceability Platform
22e46c8f-cf7e-4d53-b5f2-56c939ee76ff.description: A dedicated traceability platform focuses specifically on lot genealogy and the critical tracking event (CTE) / key data element (KDE) capture that the FSMA Food Traceability Rule (FSMA 204) requires for foods on the Food Traceability List. Rather than trying to retrofit ERP or WMS to hold FSMA 204-specific data structures, plants handling covered foods often layer a purpose-built platform on top that ingests CTE/KDE data from those systems and exposes it in the standardized form regulators and trading partners expect, frequently built on the GS1 EPCIS event model so that traceability data can be exchanged across supply-chain partners on shared standards rather than proprietary formats.

capabilities.name: Lot genealogy tracking
capabilities.description: Captures parent-child relationships between lots as materials move through receiving, transformation, and shipping, so any finished lot can be traced back to its component inputs.

capabilities.name: CTE (Critical Tracking Event) capture
capabilities.description: Records the key supply-chain events FSMA 204 requires - growing, receiving, transformation, creation, and shipping - at the point each occurs.

capabilities.name: KDE (Key Data Element) recordkeeping
capabilities.description: Stores the specific data points tied to each CTE (e.g. lot code, quantity, unit of measure, location, date/time, source) in the structured form regulators expect.

capabilities.name: GS1 EPCIS event modeling
capabilities.description: Structures traceability events using the GS1 EPCIS (Electronic Product Code Information Services) standard so events are interoperable with trading partners and industry traceability networks.

capabilities.name: Recall/lookback query support
capabilities.description: Enables rapid forward-and-backward tracing from a given lot or shipment to support recall scope determination and lookback investigations.

e7cf071d-ca71-42ea-81de-4924de27f07c.name: Label Management Software
e7cf071d-ca71-42ea-81de-4924de27f07c.description: Label management software handles the workflow around getting label artwork from initial copy draft to a compliant, approved, print-ready file: version control on artwork iterations, routing for regulatory/compliance review against nutrient-content and allergen labeling rules, and maintaining an audit trail of who approved which version and when. The strongest implementations link directly into the formulation/recipe database so that a formula change (a new ingredient, an updated allergen) automatically flags any label artwork built on the old formula as needing re-review, closing the gap where a recipe changes but nobody remembers to update the label.

capabilities.name: Version Control
capabilities.description: Tracks label artwork revisions over time, maintaining a history of changes and preventing outdated versions from being used in production.

capabilities.name: Compliance Review Routing
capabilities.description: Routes label artwork through required review steps (e.g., regulatory, quality, legal) before approval, ensuring the correct reviewers sign off before a label can proceed.

capabilities.name: Approval Audit Trail
capabilities.description: Records who approved each label version, when, and under what review cycle, creating a defensible record for regulatory or customer audits.

capabilities.name: Formulation/Recipe Database Linkage
capabilities.description: Ideally connects label content (ingredient statements, allergen declarations, nutrition facts) to the underlying formulation or recipe record so label claims stay synchronized with actual product composition.

7e49ad00-a211-4140-a869-d669d929d49e.name: Production Scheduling System
7e49ad00-a211-4140-a869-d669d929d49e.description: A production scheduling system (often called Advanced Planning & Scheduling, or APS) sits above simple ERP order sequencing to solve the harder combinatorial problem of ordering production runs across constraints that interact with each other: available equipment capacity, changeover time between runs (which is especially costly when it involves an allergen changeover requiring full cleaning and verification), raw material availability and shelf life, and outbound delivery deadlines. Scheduling allergen changeovers well is a particularly food-and-beverage-specific problem, since sequencing runs from fewer-allergen to more-allergen products (rather than the reverse) can reduce the cleaning burden between runs; this system is typically where that sequencing logic gets applied at the schedule level, working from the ERP-level Master Production Schedule down to an executable line-by-line sequence.

capabilities.name: Capacity Planning
capabilities.description: Models available equipment/line capacity and throughput constraints to determine feasible production schedules.

capabilities.name: Changeover Optimization
capabilities.description: Sequences production runs to minimize changeover time, including allergen changeovers that require validated cleaning/clearance steps between runs.

capabilities.name: Raw Material Availability & Shelf-Life Tracking
capabilities.description: Checks on-hand and incoming raw material quantities and shelf-life windows to ensure scheduled runs have usable inputs at production time.

capabilities.name: Delivery Deadline Alignment
capabilities.description: Sequences and prioritizes production orders against customer or distribution delivery deadlines.

d08beab6-1d89-42e5-8f51-c12ddc9dcc93.name: Formulation / Recipe Management System
d08beab6-1d89-42e5-8f51-c12ddc9dcc93.description: A formulation/recipe management system is the single source of truth for a product's exact composition, held as a version-controlled bill of materials rather than a static document. Every downstream calculation that depends on formula composition - nutrition facts panel generation, allergen declaration, cost rollups, and MRP demand for raw materials - pulls from this system rather than each maintaining its own separate copy, so that a single formula change propagates consistently everywhere it matters instead of requiring parallel manual updates across nutrition, labeling, and planning teams. Version control matters specifically because a plant needs to know which formula version was actually in effect for a given historical batch, not just what the current formula is.

capabilities.name: Recipe/BOM authoring
capabilities.description: Structured definition of a product's formulation as a bill of materials — ingredients, sub-recipes, and quantities/percentages that make up the finished product.

capabilities.name: Version control
capabilities.description: Tracks formulation changes over time so the current and historical composition of any product can be reconstructed, supporting reformulations and audit trail.

capabilities.name: Nutrition calculation
capabilities.description: Derives nutrition facts panel values from the underlying formulation by rolling up ingredient-level nutrient data.

capabilities.name: Allergen declaration
capabilities.description: Propagates allergen status from component ingredients up through the BOM to generate the finished product's allergen declarations.

capabilities.name: Cost rollup
capabilities.description: Calculates finished product cost by aggregating ingredient and sub-recipe costs according to the formulation.

capabilities.name: MRP feed
capabilities.description: Supplies the formulation/BOM data to materials requirements planning so ingredient purchasing and production planning reflect the current recipe.

0895906c-34f8-43a0-a4d8-112d3a9eb395.name: EDI (via VAN or AS2)
0895906c-34f8-43a0-a4d8-112d3a9eb395.description: EDI is the long-established mechanism for exchanging standard business documents directly computer-to-computer between trading partners, replacing manual entry of purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices with structured X12 transaction sets transmitted over a Value Added Network (a third-party intermediary that routes and translates EDI traffic between partners) or a direct AS2 connection (point-to-point transmission over the internet with built-in encryption and delivery receipts). The transaction sets most common in food and beverage supply chains are the 850 (Purchase Order), 855 (Purchase Order Acknowledgment), 856 (Advance Ship Notice), 810 (Invoice), 940 (Warehouse Shipping Order), and 945 (Warehouse Shipping Advice). In this domain, EDI is typically the transport layer underneath documents like the purchase order and advance ship notice - the business document's content and structure are what the transaction set numbers define, while EDI itself is just how that structured data physically moves between the buyer's and seller's systems.

capabilities.name: Purchase Order (850)
capabilities.description: Sent by a buyer/customer to place an order specifying items, quantities, and requested delivery terms.

capabilities.name: Purchase Order Acknowledgment (855)
capabilities.description: Sent by the supplier to confirm, reject, or propose changes to a received 850 order.

capabilities.name: Advance Ship Notice (856)
capabilities.description: Sent ahead of a shipment detailing carton/pallet contents and expected delivery, typically paired with barcode labels used at receiving.

capabilities.name: Invoice (810)
capabilities.description: Sent by the supplier to bill the buyer for goods shipped, referencing the corresponding purchase order and shipment.

capabilities.name: Warehouse Shipping Order (940)
capabilities.description: Sent by a shipper instructing a third-party warehouse to pick and ship specific goods.

capabilities.name: Warehouse Shipping Advice (945)
capabilities.description: Sent by the warehouse back to the shipper confirming what was actually picked and shipped against a 940 order.

capabilities.name: Transport method
capabilities.description: The connection mechanism used to exchange these documents computer-to-computer, either through a Value-Added Network (VAN) intermediary or a direct AS2 connection.

9831aefd-1733-472a-b8e0-954c68512132.name: Training / LMS (Learning Management System)
9831aefd-1733-472a-b8e0-954c68512132.description: A Training/LMS platform manages the assignment, completion tracking, and certification recordkeeping for food-safety and GMP training — the digital system of record behind the Training / Qualification Record artifact. It exists as a distinct category from QMS document control because assignment/completion/recertification tracking across a whole workforce, often against role-specific curricula, is a different problem than managing HACCP plan documents or CAPA workflows, even though the two systems' outputs (training evidence, food-safety records) both end up supporting the same audits.

capabilities.name: Training assignment / curriculum management
capabilities.description: Maps required courses, modules, or curricula to individuals or roles and schedules recurring or triggered assignments.

capabilities.name: Completion tracking
capabilities.description: Records when each assigned training item is started and completed, including pass/fail or attendance status, and flags overdue or missing completions.

capabilities.name: Competency assessment
capabilities.description: Captures quiz, test, or observed skill-check results tied to a training module, used to verify understanding rather than mere attendance.

capabilities.name: Certification / credential recordkeeping
capabilities.description: Stores issued certificates, expiration dates, and renewal requirements for credentials such as HACCP certification, allergen training, or equipment-specific qualifications.

capabilities.name: Audit-ready reporting
capabilities.description: Generates exportable records of who was trained on what and when, formatted to support regulatory inspection or customer audit requests for training documentation.

2227d2ed-2fec-40de-a9d4-52e262f14809.name: SCADA / HMI
2227d2ed-2fec-40de-a9d4-52e262f14809.description: SCADA/HMI is the direct equipment-control and data-acquisition layer that talks to PLCs and line sensors in real time, giving operators the screens they actually use to run and monitor a line. ISA-95 (IEC 62264) formally separates this layer (Level 2, basic process control) from MES (Level 3, manufacturing operations management) — MES's own record in this dataset already describes talking to plant-floor automation such as PLCs and SCADA, which is exactly this layer, but no record covered it directly until now. In practice, SCADA/HMI is where a process setup gets dialed in and where a CCP's raw sensor reading first becomes a number an operator can see, before MES aggregates it into a batch record.

capabilities.name: Data acquisition
capabilities.description: Continuous polling/collection of real-time process values from PLCs, sensors, and field instrumentation.

capabilities.name: Human-machine interface (HMI)
capabilities.description: Operator-facing screens for visualizing live equipment/process status and issuing manual control commands.

capabilities.name: Alarm and event management
capabilities.description: Threshold-based alerting and event logging when process values move outside normal operating ranges.

capabilities.name: Control interface to PLCs
capabilities.description: The real-time communication layer (e.g., OPC, Modbus, proprietary protocols) that reads from and writes control commands to programmable logic controllers.

capabilities.name: Historian / trending
capabilities.description: Time-series storage of acquired data supporting trend charts and short-to-medium-term historical review at the equipment level.

capabilities.name: Level 2/3 interface
capabilities.description: The upward data hand-off point where SCADA/HMI (ISA-95 Level 2) feeds aggregated production data to MES (Level 3).
